runs.interpretation: Global labor-supply income-response elasticity used in a U.S. tax-benefit microsimulation model for adults under current-law policy.
runs.reasoningSummary: I endorse a modest positive income elasticity for labor supply in simulation settings, reflecting that higher disposable income generally raises labor supply (but with substantial heterogeneity and many designs that often yield small-to-moderate average responses). The lower tail allows near-zero effects, while the upper tail allows stronger responses for subpopulations closer to intensive-margin adjustments.
